commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2e219cf8ba7f004d41856b3d97e6ac5d75d1a0a1)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a heuristic lightweight reply lane for eligible plain text replies, a five-minute Codex initial-progress watchdog, and focused tests plus Codex shard configuration.

PR surface: Source +260, Tests +295. Total +555 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the classifier admits short texts by denylist fallback, and Codex upstream emits turn/started before prewarm resolves. I did not run a live Telegram or Codex timeout reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer review and contributor proof because the remaining blockers are default reply-routing policy and Codex timeout semantics, not a safe single mechanical repair.

target.title: feat(auto-reply,codex): lightweight reply lane and Codex watchdog
target.body:
## Summary
- add a conservative lightweight reply lane for obvious low-risk text-only chat replies
- keep uncertain, tool/action, media/link, high-stakes, code/config, unresolved reply-target, and configured skill-filter messages on the full path
- add a Codex initial-progress watchdog that caps the accepted-but-silent gap at 5 minutes, clamped to the configured attempt timeout, and restores the full timeout once item-level progress arrives
- assign the new Codex attempt notification-state test to the explicit app-server attempt support shard

## What Problem This Solves
Normal conversational replies were paying the full setup and tool-wiring cost even when no tools were needed, while accepted-but-silent Codex turns could wait the entire attempt timeout before surfacing as stalled. This PR keeps uncertain or policy-constrained messages on the full path, while giving plain chat and silent-start Codex turns a narrower, observable path.

## Why
- normal conversational replies should not pay the full setup/tool wiring cost when no tools are needed
- over-escalation remains harmless because borderline or policy-constrained messages stay on the full agent path
- Codex runs that are accepted but silent should not wait the entire attempt timeout before surfacing as stalled

## Evidence
- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenClaw Telegram replies now have a conservative lightweight admission lane, and Codex accepted-but-silent turns have a short initial-progress watchdog instead of waiting the whole attempt timeout.
- Real environment tested: Mantis captured native Telegram Desktop before/after proof for this PR on the previous PR head, showing a plain chat reply delivered on both main and the candidate.
- Mantis proof: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93244#issuecomment-4816610857
- Latest contributor fix on this head: lightweight admission now receives the merged skill policy before deciding whether to disable tools, so agent-configured skill filters block the lightweight lane.
- Local proof on latest head:
  ```bash
  pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex-app-server-attempt-support.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts -t "covers each normal full-suite test file exactly once"
  pnpm check:test-types
  ```
- Observed result after fix: formatting passed; focused lightweight/get-reply/reply-admission/Codex notification-state tests passed; the full-suite coverage assertion that previously failed for the new notification-state test passed; test types passed.
- What is still pending: refreshed hosted GitHub checks and any refreshed Mantis/ClawSweeper verdict for the latest head.

## Tests
- `pnpm format:check src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex-app-server-attempt-support.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/lightweight-lane.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts -t "covers each normal full-suite test file exactly once"`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
